text.title: Launch Tier Decision Frame Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Launch Tier Decision Frame application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Tier ≥2 MUST clear readiness gate (capacity OK + support headroom ≥4h/day + rollback path); failing tier ≥2 is rejected.
rule.rationale: Launches that exceed readiness produce churn, refund storms, and bad press.

rule.statement: Selected tier MUST match named funnel goal (PH→signups, podcast→trust, HN→technical, friend→qualitative).
rule.rationale: Channel-funnel mismatch wastes the launch slot.

rule.statement: Decision MUST name retreat cost in concrete units (refund window, signup-graveyard size, PR risk).
Decision MUST select exactly ONE tier; multi-tier 'all of the above' is rejected.
rule.rationale: All-of-the-above is a non-decision; force commitment.

rule.statement: Decision record MUST carry version + rationale paragraph (≥200 chars); empty rationale rejected.
rule.rationale: Un-rationaled decisions cannot be reviewed post-launch.
